invariants: Finite extensions are not promoted to an all-k theorem.
The scaled-defect nonincreasing shortcut remains rejected.
The one-third-width buffer is not promoted after the k150 frontier failure.
The fixed half-width buffer is finite-rejected after the k150 frontier failure.
Endpoint PF, RH, Laguerre-Polya membership, and Lambda <= 0 are forbidden as inputs.
proof_boundary: Open theorem target only. It states the all-k defect-tail theorem needed after the finite negative-lambda prefix, but it does not prove the tail theorem, does not prove zeta cone entry, does not prove jwpf_06, and does not prove RH or Lambda <= 0.

summary.main_finding: The defect-tail target is now indexed precisely: finite evidence covers x_1..x_149, so an analytic proof must supply 0 <= d_k <= 2/(2*k+1) for k >= 150 and d_(k+1) <= d_k for k >= 149. Two live routes remain: uniform saddle/Laplace control and a direct ratio-recurrence comparison. The scaled-defect nonincreasing shortcut is rejected by the finite diagnostic; the one-third buffer is too strong on the k150 prefix, and the fixed half-width buffer is also finite-rejected.

target_rows.acceptance_test: Provide noncircular estimates for the actual zeta heat-flow coefficients, with explicit starting index and no endpoint PF/RH input.
target_rows.claim_if_proved: For each checked negative lambda, prove 0 <= d_k <= 2/(2*k+1) for all k >= 150 and d_(k+1) <= d_k for all k >= 149, where d_k=1-x_k.
target_rows.gap: No analytic all-k defect-tail theorem is currently proved.
target_rows.proof_boundary: Open theorem target only; not a proof of cone entry.

target_rows.acceptance_test: State whether the route proves the bridge inequality analytically or adds the missing coefficient enclosure.
target_rows.claim_if_proved: The existing finite prefix supplies x_1..x_149; a tail theorem starting at x_150 plus the bridge inequality d_150 <= d_149 would complete the infinite ratio-cone entry on the checked lambda grid.
target_rows.gap: The bridge value x_150 would otherwise require A_151.
target_rows.proof_boundary: Requirement extraction only; not a tail theorem.

target_rows.acceptance_test: Give explicit k-uniform remainder terms strong enough to prove the defect barriers from the stated starting index.
target_rows.claim_if_proved: A uniform large-negative-lambda saddle/Laplace expansion controls d_k and d_(k+1)-d_k beyond the finite anchor.
target_rows.gap: The current Phi Taylor sign certificate is fixed-k/local; it does not bound the saddle remainder uniformly in k.

target_rows.acceptance_test: Derive an inequality involving only already-controlled tail quantities and verify its base case noncircularly.
target_rows.claim_if_proved: A direct recurrence or comparison inequality for x_k proves positivity, the lower wall, and monotone defect decrease for the actual coefficient tail.
target_rows.gap: No closed recurrence or comparison principle has been proved for the zeta heat-flow coefficient tail; the width-preserving recurrence, the one-third buffer, and the fixed half-width buffer are now finite-rejected on the k150 prefix.

target_rows.acceptance_test: Do not use scaled-defect nonincrease as a proof premise.
target_rows.claim_if_proved: The shortcut s_k=((2*k+1)/2)*d_k is nonincreasing would imply the lower wall from one finite bound.
target_rows.gap: The certified finite prefix shows s_k is increasing on every checked adjacent pair.
target_rows.proof_boundary: Rejected by finite diagnostic evidence only.

target_rows.acceptance_test: Use finite extensions as diagnostics or base cases only, unless paired with a genuine analytic tail theorem.
target_rows.claim_if_proved: More ACB coefficients can keep extending the finite collar depth.
target_rows.gap: Finite extension alone does not prove the all-k tail or an analytic collared finite flow theorem.

target_rows.acceptance_test: Reject any route that imports endpoint PF/LP/RH/Newman conclusions as premises.
target_rows.claim_if_proved: Endpoint Jensen hyperbolicity, Laguerre-Polya membership, RH, or Lambda <= 0 would imply strong tail inequalities.
target_rows.gap: Those endpoint statements are outputs or stronger assumptions, not admissible inputs for this Newman-direction programme.

target_rows.acceptance_test: After a proof, re-run the cone-entry target and dependency graph without changing forbidden endpoint assumptions.
target_rows.claim_if_proved: The finite prefix plus this all-k defect-tail theorem would supply the missing cone-entry input for the conditional heat-flow ratio-cone route.
target_rows.gap: This would still need the surrounding infinite/collared flow legitimacy and downstream Jensen-window PF bridge checks.
target_rows.proof_boundary: Conditional application only; not jwpf_06 or Lambda <= 0.
